dependencies: Transmission and distribution infrastructure concentrated in Southern California
Nuclear fuel supply for Palo Verde Nuclear Generating Station
Natural gas supply from SoCalGas for generation facilities
Dependence on contractors for decommissioning activities at San Onofre
Reliance on contract workers including Safety Tier 1 Contractors for operations
risks: Supply chain constraints impacting capital project timelines
Availability and cost of spare parts for equipment
Delays in permitting and regulatory approvals affecting project execution
Dependence on decommissioning general contractor (DGC) for San Onofre activities
Risk of contractor safety incidents and compliance failures
Elevated inflation impacting construction and material costs
Tariff impacts on equipment and materials procurement

regulatory_risks: CPUC rate recovery denials or disallowances for wildfire-related costs
Failure to obtain CPUC approval for capital expenditures and cost recovery
CPUC prudency determinations under AB 1054 framework for wildfire costs
FERC rate recovery and prudency reviews for transmission costs
NRC regulatory requirements for nuclear decommissioning at San Onofre
NERC reliability and critical infrastructure protection standards compliance
OEIS safety certification requirements and wildfire mitigation plan approvals
Environmental regulatory compliance including GHG emissions regulations
California RPS (Renewables Portfolio Standard) compliance requirements
Penalties and fines from CPUC for safety violations and non-compliance
CPUC citation authority up to $100,000 per violation per day (capped at $8 million)
NERC penalties up to $1.5 million per violation per day for reliability standard violations
Customer affordability concerns impacting rate increase approvals
Changes in California environmental priorities and climate policies
New regulations on data privacy and cybersecurity
Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) regulations
Permitting delays from multiple federal, state, and local agencies
California Coastal Commission and environmental agency approvals for projects
FERC transmission planning and competitive solicitation requirements
Changes in tax laws and regulations at state and federal levels
Potential changes to AB 1054 prudency standard interpretation by CPUC
Risk of CPUC disallowing costs above authorized levels in GRC proceedings
Regulatory restrictions on PSPS (Public Safety Power Shutoff) operations
CPUC enforcement actions and Administrative Consent Orders
Environmental remediation cost recovery subject to reasonableness review
Nuclear decommissioning cost recovery subject to CPUC NDCTP reviews
Wildfire Insurance Fund exhaustion and reimbursement limitations
AB 1054 Liability Cap limitations on fund reimbursement
Regulatory changes to net energy metering (NEM) and customer generation policies
CPUC decisions on cost allocation and rate design impacting cost recovery
